3.happening: Creating the empty result list for finished combinations.
3.why: Every valid combination discovered will be collected here.
3.invariant: result holds only combinations that sum to the target.

4.happening: Creating the empty path — the combination being built right now.
4.why: We push and pop candidates on path as we explore.
4.invariant: path plus remaining always accounts for the full target.

5.happening: Starting the search from candidate 0 with the full target {target} remaining.
5.why: Backtracking will explore every choice of candidates from here.
5.invariant: The search begins with an empty path.

6.why: The search has explored and unwound every branch.
6.invariant: result contains exactly the combinations summing to the target.

9.happening: Remaining is 0 — the path sums exactly to the target!
9.why: A remaining of zero means the chosen numbers hit the target.
9.invariant: path is now a valid combination.

9.happening: Remaining is {remaining}, not zero — keep exploring.
9.why: The path does not sum to the target yet.
9.invariant: More candidates may still complete it.

10.why: A snapshot copy is saved so later pops do not change it.

11.why: Once a combination is complete, this branch is done.
11.invariant: The caller will undo its last choice and try the next candidate.

12.happening: Remaining is {remaining}, below zero — this branch overshot.
12.why: Adding any more candidates can only overshoot further.
12.invariant: A negative remaining can never reach the target.

12.happening: Remaining is {remaining}, still positive — this branch is alive.
12.why: There is still room to add more candidates.
12.invariant: Only a non-negative remaining can still succeed.

13.why: No combination down this path can sum to the target.
13.invariant: Pruned branches add nothing to result.

14.why: Starting at {start} avoids counting the same combination in a different order.
14.invariant: Only candidates at or after start are considered here.

15.happening: Choosing candidate {candidates[i]} — pushing it onto the path.
15.why: We commit to this number and will reduce the remaining target by it.

16.happening: Recursing with {remaining} - {candidates[i]} = {remaining - candidates[i]} remaining, candidate {candidates[i]} still allowed.
16.why: The same candidate may repeat, so the recursion starts again at i.
16.invariant: The deeper call explores extensions of the current path.

17.happening: Backtracking: popping candidate {candidates[i]} off the path.
17.why: We undo the last choice so the loop can try the next candidate.
17.invariant: path returns to its state before this choice.

init.happening: Setting up the empty result and path.
init.why: Backtracking needs somewhere to build and to collect combinations.
init.invariant: path is the current combination; result holds finished ones.

recurse.why: Each recursive call fixes one more candidate in the combination.
recurse.invariant: The call stack mirrors the current path.

loop.why: Every candidate from start onward is a possible next number.

check.happening: Testing whether the path hit or overshot the target.
check.why: Zero remaining is a solution; negative is a dead end.

update.happening: Choosing, recording, or undoing a candidate.
update.why: Backtracking commits to each choice and later reverts it.
update.invariant: path grows and shrinks by one candidate at a time.

return.happening: Ending a branch and handing results upward.
return.why: A branch ends on success, on a prune, or when candidates run out.
